title: How Lebanon’s Best Chance to Disarm Hezbollah Failed
summary: Lebanon’s government has long wanted the powerful militia to give up its weapons. Before the Iran war began, there were signs of progress toward that goal.

title: Netanyahu Orders Israeli Military to Attack Beirut Suburbs
summary: Thousands fled the southern suburbs of Lebanon’s capital after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel announced the widening of the military campaign against Hezbollah.

title: Losing Trust in Justice Dept., Judges Call Out Its Lawyers’ Behavior
summary: The federal courts have long assumed that the government’s lawyers are trustworthy. Now judges across the country are criticizing their lack of candor.

title: Hegseth Strikes Female and Black Navy Officers From Promotion List
summary: The defense secretary’s decision to block the officers’ promotions appears driven by his anti-diversity stance rather than based on merit.

title: They Voted for Trump. Here’s How They Feel About High Gas Prices.
summary: With the midterm elections five months away, many Americans who backed President Trump are wrestling with pain at the pump and the war in Iran.

title: Anthropic Files to Go Public, Setting Stage for Huge I.P.O.
summary: The artificial intelligence company, which is racing rival OpenAI to the stock market, has seen explosive growth over the last year thanks largely to technology that can automatically write computer code.

title: What It’s Like to Be a Student at the First A.I.-Powered University
summary: California’s public university system spent $16.9 million on A.I. The result has been chaos.

title: Florida Sues OpenAI Over Chatbot Safety Concerns
summary: The state became the first to sue the ChatGPT maker over claims that its technology posed a risk to children and that the company had failed to warn the public of dangers.

title: The Return of Blaming and Shaming in Public Health
summary: For years, medicine has tried to eliminate stigma. Now, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is bringing back the language of personal responsibility.

title: Sam Levinson on Why ‘Euphoria’ Had to End This Way. For Good.
summary: In an exclusive interview, Sam Levinson explained why he felt it was time to bring the series to a close and responded to its detractors.

title: In the ‘Euphoria’ Finale, the HBO Show Goes Out With a Whimper
summary: In the end, a show defined by its excesses seemed to be straining for a redemption that it didn’t need.

title: Are Texans Ready for Talarico’s Kind of Christianity?
summary: Jim Rigby, a pastor who rarely uses the word “God,” is a key to understanding the Senate candidate trying to pull off something unusual in Texas.

title: As Democrats Worry About Senate Race, Platner Attacks Reports About Sexual Messages
summary: Graham Platner, whose contest in Maine is a key to Democrats’ hopes of winning the Senate, sought to discredit reports that he had exchanged sexual messages with women outside his marriage.

title: Alberta’s Movement to Separate from Canada Gets Its Moment
summary: In October, Albertans will get to say if they want to stay in Canada, or hold a referendum to leave. Will it settle the matter, or deepen the rift?

title: Joana Avillez Revisits Her Waterfront Childhood
summary: Joana Avillez took six years to illustrate a new edition of Joseph Mitchell’s “The Bottom of the Harbor,” which captures the salty New York neighborhood of her youth.

title: Inside the NYC Primary Between Antonio Reynoso and Claire Valdez
summary: The Democratic battle over a House seat in New York City has reignited old tensions over gentrification, identity and who gets to define the left.

title: What to Watch For in the NY Primary: Key Races and Why They Matter
summary: Congressional primaries in Manhattan, Brooklyn and beyond could test the strength of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s socialist movement and reverberate across the country.

title: N.Y.C. Children May Get Up to $3,000 From City for College Accounts
summary: The city’s current savings plan starts with $100. A City Council proposal would make the initial contributions among the highest anywhere.

title: China Aims A.I. at Predicting Who Could Pose a Political Risk
summary: New research examines how a Chinese company struggled to develop its predictive surveillance technology while U.S. restrictions were in place.

title: How an Act of Vandalism Helped a Dissident Find His Voice
summary: Li Ying, known as Teacher Li to his 2.2 million followers on X, doesn’t live in China but still faces smear campaigns and death threats. He is not letting that stop him.

title: Prized by ‘MAHA’ Influencers and Chefs Alike, Craft Flour Is on the Rise
summary: Growing demand for fiber-rich grains is boosting the premium flour industry, which offers less processed, but pricier, alternatives to the classic grocery store brands.

title: Marco Rubio: Florida Centrist, Tea Party Darling, MAGA Warrior, President?
summary: The secret to the Secretary of State’s success.

title: Why This Summer Travel Season Will Be a Nightmare
summary: Air travel could be disrupted into next year because of high jet fuel prices.

title: Death from Above
summary: We look at U.S. boat strikes in the Caribbean Sea and eastern Pacific Ocean.

title: An Industrial Gem in Venezuela Now Embodies the Country’s Decay
summary: Cumaná was once an economic hub, producing Toyota Land Cruisers and exporting food across South America. Now it’s on the brink of disaster as public services collapse.
